schema.name: TZPID source truth registry gap fill
schema.basis: folder-local candidate promotion with safety gate

theory.canonical_statement: The standard answer invokes conservation of angular momentum: a collapsing gas cloud spins faster as it contracts, like a figure skater pulling in her arms, conserving the angular momentum it started with. The spin of planets, stars, and galaxies is inherited from the primordial angular momentum of the matter that formed them. The Gyromagnetic Genesis Hypothesis rejects this premise. Angular momentum is not inherited from a primordial reservoir — it is continuously generated, here and now, by local electromagnetic coupling. Rotation is not a conserved relic of the Big Bang; it is an ongoing process, regenerated at every moment by the interaction between magnetic fields and the matter that carries them.
theory.technical_interpretation: ic field $\mathbf{B}$ and the magnetic moment $\boldsymbol{\mu}$ of the matter. The coupling Hamiltonian: The first term is the standard Zeeman coupling. The second term — with coupling constant $\lambda_g$ (the gyromagnetic coupling, ID 045) — couples the mechanical angular momentum $\mathbf{L}$ directly to the magnetic field. This is the genesis term: **a magnetic field exerts a torque on matter, generating angular momentum.** The dimensionless gyromagnetic coupling constant (ID 045): measures the strength of this genesis relative to the natural rotation frequency. When $\Gamma \sim 1$, the magnetic field can generate rotation at a rate comparable to the existing rotation — and the system enters the self-sustaining regime. ### The Seed Rotation at Emergence At the moment a system emerges from the TZP (the lemniscate bifurcation), it acquires a seed rotation $L_0$ and seed magnetic mome In technical terms, the entry packages the selected formula as the operative carrier, the nearby equations as the progression layer, and the symbol key as the compact interpretation of variables used by the source.

reviewed_source_symbols.description: entry-local variable used by the Gyromagnetic Genesis relation and interpreted through the cited equation context.
magnetic field or geometric ball term in the equation.
angular frequency controlling the oscillatory part of the relation.
cosmological, spectral, or coupling constant used by the entry context.
mass, chemical potential, permeability, or measure parameter from the source context.
density term for matter, probability, charge, or field support in the cited relation.

dictionary_sections.definition: Gyromagnetic Genesis is a source-backed Opera Omnia registry packet centered on the canonical relation \Gamma = \frac{\lambda_g B}{\omega_0}. It identifies the mathematical spine of the cited chapter and gives the entry a concrete equation, symbol key, and derivation chain for the four-page production layout.
dictionary_sections.contextual_meaning: The standard answer invokes conservation of angular momentum: a collapsing gas cloud spins faster as it contracts, like a figure skater pulling in her arms, conserving the angular momentum it started with. The spin of planets, stars, and galaxies is inherited from the primordial angular momentum of the matter that formed them. The Gyromagnetic Genesis Hypothesis rejects this premise. Angular momentum is not inherited from a primordial reservoir — it is continuously generated, here and now, by local electromagnetic coupling. Rotation is not a conserved relic of the Big Bang; it is an ongoing process, regenerated at every moment by the interaction between magnetic fields and the matter that carries them. The entry should be read as part of the larger Opera Omnia progression: a local equation is selected, its surrounding prose supplies the physical or mathematical role, and the production packet keeps that source connection visible.
dictionary_sections.derivable_consequences: The equation progression records \Gamma = \frac{\lambda_g B}{\omega_0}; \Lambda = \frac{B^2}{\mu_0\rho\eta\omega}; \hat{H}_\text{coupling} = -\boldsymbol{\mu}\cdot\mathbf{B} + \lambda_g\,\mathbf{L}\cdot\mathbf{B}; R_m = \frac{vL}{\eta} > R_m^\text{crit} \approx 10. These relations give the support page enough structure to show how the canonical formula arises from nearby source material rather than from a disconnected title alone.
dictionary_sections.lemma_support: The supporting lemma is source locality: the selected relation appears in Gyromagnetic_Genesis_EDITED.md, and the adjacent context connects it to the title, domain, and surrounding argument. The packet therefore treats the formula, its explanatory paragraph, and its neighboring equations as one evidence cluster.

dictionary_sections.equation_grounding: Grounded source: `D:\Conversation\4-Ready-For-ProofReading\Gyromagnetic_Genesis_EDITED.md`. Canonical relation: \Gamma = \frac{\lambda_g B}{\omega_0}. The equation support section is drawn from the same proofread file and ordered by source strength.
dictionary_sections.interpretive_role: Gyromagnetic Genesis extends the registry beyond the first thousand IDs by giving the new band a cited mathematical object, a text-rich dictionary page, and a derivation path attached to the exact source file.
dictionary_sections.tzp_thesaurus:
\begin{enumerate}[leftmargin=1.6em,itemsep=6pt,topsep=4pt]
\item Canonical carrier: the selected formula that anchors the entry.
\item Source context: the chapter prose that explains why the formula belongs in the registry.
\item TRAWIN closure: the reading of the formula through time, rotation, action, wave, integration, and normalization.
\end{enumerate}

derivation_layer.source_latex:
% BEGIN TZP DERIVATION LAYER
\tzpsection{Derivation Layer}
\begin{enumerate}[leftmargin=1.6em,itemsep=7pt,topsep=4pt]
\item
\[
\Gamma = \frac{\lambda_g B}{\omega_0}
\]
This line is the 1 source equation retained from Gyromagnetic\_Genesis\_EDITED.md for the Gyromagnetic Genesis packet.
\item
\[
\Lambda = \frac{B^2}{\mu_0\rho\eta\omega}
\]
This line is the 2 source equation retained from Gyromagnetic\_Genesis\_EDITED.md for the Gyromagnetic Genesis packet.
\item
\[
\hat{H}_\text{coupling} = -\boldsymbol{\mu}\cdot\mathbf{B} + \lambda_g\,\mathbf{L}\cdot\mathbf{B}
\]
This line is the 3 source equation retained from Gyromagnetic\_Genesis\_EDITED.md for the Gyromagnetic Genesis packet.
\item
\[
R_m = \frac{vL}{\eta} > R_m^\text{crit} \approx 10
\]
This line is the 4 source equation retained from Gyromagnetic\_Genesis\_EDITED.md for the Gyromagnetic Genesis packet.
\item
\[
\mu \propto M R^2 \omega
\]
This line is the 5 source equation retained from Gyromagnetic\_Genesis\_EDITED.md for the Gyromagnetic Genesis packet.
\end{enumerate}
% END TZP DERIVATION LAYER
